config.rationale: Experiment 6/30 — pivot from MTP attempts (NEXTN failed 2x consecutively on SGLang, Rule 10 says stop) and from --language-model-only ablation (delivered only ~2.4% TTFT win and slight throughput regression). Goal is optimize_throughput, so this run stacks the throughput-oriented vLLM knobs the previous baselines deliberately left off, on top of the current throughput champion (8e036532f8f1, 7098.5 tok/s). Changes vs that baseline: (1) enable_chunked_prefill=true — overlaps prefill with decode batches, the single biggest throughput lever for vLLM at high concurrency; (2) enable_prefix_caching=true — common-prompt reuse, typically +5–20% on benchmark workloads with shared prefixes; (3) max_num_seqs=512 (vLLM default 256) — allows wider decode batches on H100 where memory is plentiful; (4) max_num_batched_tokens=16384 — large enough for chunked prefill to feed decode efficiently without starving; (5) gpu_memory_utilization bumped 0.9→0.92 to claim a bit more KV cache headroom freed by --language-model-only (vision tower skipped saves ~2 GB); (6) keep --language-model-only since it's a free win on a text-only benchmark and frees vision tower memory. max_model_len held at 65536 to stay apples-to-apples with prior baselines and avoid OOM risk from the bigger batch envelope. dtype/kv_cache_dtype=auto, fcfs, no quantization, no spec decoding — preserving correctness while isolating the batching+caching effect. If this clears 7098 tok/s cleanly, follow-up can try fp8 KV cache for further KV-bound throughput gains; if TTFT regresses we'll know chunked_prefill chunk size is too small.

smoke_tests.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)

llm_commentary: Experiment 6 set a new throughput record at 7142.1 tok/s (+0.6% over the prior 8e036532f8f1 baseline) by stacking chunked_prefill, prefix_caching, max_num_seqs=512, and a 16k batched-token budget — modest but real gains. TTFT p95 rose to 57.1ms from the 44.5ms latency leader, so it loses on pure latency but still well under the 500ms balanced threshold, making it the new balanced champion as well. Smoke tests are clean (only json_mode fails, consistent with prior reasoning-model behavior). The throughput uplift was smaller than the rationale anticipated, suggesting headroom remains on the KV-cache and batching fronts.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-vllm-94f131fd3918 --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d vllm/vllm-openai:latest --model Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tensor-parallel-size 1 --dtype auto --gpu-memory-utilization 0.92 --max-model-len 65536 --max-num-seqs 512 --max-num-batched-tokens 16384 --enable-chunked-prefill --enable-prefix-caching --language-model-only --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder --reasoning-parser qwen3
